Effective tax rate increased from 15.4% to 17.1% primarily due to decreased stock-based compensation tax benefits and non-deductible legal settlement. New U.S. federal tax law changes signed July 2025 may affect consolidated financial statements beginning Q3 2025.
periodic.forward_outlook: Revenue backlog reached $108.2 billion with approximately 55% expected to be recognized over next 24 months, primarily from Google Cloud commitments. Capital expenditures accelerated to $39.6 billion in first six months reflecting continued investment in technical infrastructure to support AI products and services. Management expects Wiz acquisition to integrate into Google Cloud segment upon closing in 2026.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Antitrust remedy proceedings present material business model risks with DOJ decisions expected August 2025 for search matters and September 2025 for advertising technology, with potential structural changes to search and advertising businesses. European Commission fines totaled €4.1 billion under appeal while privacy settlement agreements remain subject to final court approval. Long-term income taxes payable of $10.0 billion primarily relate to uncertain tax positions with ongoing regulatory investigations across multiple jurisdictions.

periodic.operating_state: Total revenue increased 22% to $47.5 billion driven by advertising revenue growth with ad impressions up 11% and average price per ad up 9% globally. Family of Apps daily active people grew 6% to 3.48 billion while Reality Labs contributed from Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses sales offset by lower Quest sales. Operating cash flows reached $49.6 billion for six months, up from $38.6 billion prior year, reflecting higher revenue generation and lower cash tax payments.
periodic.cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 12% driven by higher infrastructure costs and employee compensation, with research and development rising 23% supporting AI initiatives and Reality Labs investments. Operating margin expanded to 43% from 38% prior year, benefiting from $1.58 billion reduction in depreciation expense due to extended server useful lives to 5.5 years. Reality Labs operating losses of $4.5 billion continued reducing overall profitability despite revenue growth.
periodic.material_events: Board increased quarterly dividend 5% to $0.525 per share with share repurchases totaling $22.9 billion for six months. The company completed investment in Scale AI and approved disposal of data center assets for co-development arrangements. European Commission ruled Meta's subscription model violates DMA requirements in April 2025, which Meta has appealed, while board expanded to fifteen members with two new independent directors.
periodic.macro_exposures: European regulatory developments significantly impact advertising capabilities, with DMA compliance requiring less personalized ads for users declining subscription options beginning Q3 2025. Foreign currency translation generated $2.8 billion positive impact for six months, while iOS changes and mobile restrictions continue reducing ad targeting capabilities. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act enacted July 2025 will reduce U.S. federal cash tax payments, though global minimum tax regimes could increase effective rates.
periodic.forward_outlook: Capital expenditures reached $29.5 billion for six months with management projecting $66-72 billion for full year 2025 to support AI infrastructure and core business investments. Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase in 2025 despite continued substantial investments in metaverse and wearables development. The company maintains $28.2 billion remaining share repurchase authorization and extended tender offers for additional equity interests in unconsolidated entities.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: FTC antitrust litigation trial concluded May 2025 with decision expected second half, while multiple youth-related litigation trials scheduled November 2025 present material exposure. Tax Court issued May 2025 opinion requiring $1.48 billion higher intellectual property valuation for 2010 transfer pricing with appeal rights available. Outstanding contractual commitments total $27.95 billion primarily for infrastructure, with maximum exposure to loss in unconsolidated entities at $1.98 billion.

periodic.operating_state: Total revenues increased 16% to $11.1 billion in Q2 2025 driven by membership growth, higher pricing, and increased advertising revenue across all regions, with constant currency growth accelerating to 17% as foreign exchange headwinds moderated. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth across APAC and EMEA markets. Operating income surged 45% to $3.8 billion with operating margin expanding to 34.1% from 27.2% as revenue growth significantly outpaced cost increases.
periodic.cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 52% from 46% in the prior year as content amortization increased modestly but represented a smaller percentage of revenues due to strong revenue growth. Operating expenses grew at a slower rate than revenues, with technology and development costs rising primarily due to personnel expansion while sales and marketing increased driven by advertising sales headcount growth.
periodic.material_events: Reed Hastings transitioned from executive officer to non-executive Chairman effective April 17, 2025, while Elinor Mertz joined the Board and Audit Committee. The company repurchased $5.2 billion of common stock during the six-month period and repaid $1.8 billion in maturing debt while establishing a new $3 billion commercial paper program.
periodic.macro_exposures: Foreign exchange volatility generated $84 million in remeasurement losses on euro-denominated debt with 55% of revenue denominated in non-USD currencies. The company faces ongoing Brazilian tax authority disputes with cumulative potential exposure of approximately $600 million related to non-income tax assessments.
periodic.forward_outlook: Content obligations totaled $21.0 billion as of quarter-end reflecting continued investment in original programming across global markets. Management discontinued quarterly membership reporting to focus on revenue and operating margin as primary performance metrics. The company maintains $12.0 billion remaining under its share repurchase authorization with no specific timeline commitment.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Content obligations include additional unknown future commitments estimated at $1-4 billion over three years beyond disclosed totals. The company may be required to pay approximately $800 million in indirect tax deposits over the next twelve months exceeding typical obligations. Derivative instruments carry $543 million in pre-tax accumulated losses expected to impact earnings as hedges mature.

periodic.operating_state: Disney's Q2 2025 operations faced continued headwinds from evolving consumer preferences, with theatrical demand remaining below pre-pandemic levels and shifting content acceptance patterns. Parks operations were temporarily disrupted by Hurricane Milton causing Walt Disney World closures. International operations experienced delayed payments from certain third parties due to regional liquidity issues.
periodic.cost_structure: Operating costs increased due to substantial investments in content production, sports programming rights, and theme park attractions made before consumer acceptance certainty. Additional protective costs against security incidents, natural disasters, and cyber threats reduced operational profitability alongside third-party supplier dependencies and distribution channel contractions.
periodic.material_events: Disney completed the Hulu acquisition appraisal process on June 9, 2025, resulting in an additional $438.7 million payment to NBCUniversal beyond the initial amount paid in December 2023, with transaction closure expected by July 24, 2025. The company paused certain operations in specific regions due to sanctions and geopolitical developments.
periodic.macro_exposures: New 2025 tariffs with U.S. trading partners currently expected to have modest near-term impact but could significantly affect longer-term operations depending on implementation scope. Enhanced U.S. trade restrictions include new import prohibitions from certain regions, while state governments increased sector-specific legislation targeting streaming and online commerce affecting operational costs and international trade capabilities.
periodic.forward_outlook: Management expects deeper integration of Hulu's general entertainment content with Disney+ and future ESPN direct-to-consumer product following acquisition completion. The company anticipates continued substantial resource allocation to intellectual property protection against unauthorized use, particularly addressing AI-facilitated content infringement, while adapting to evolving consumer preferences across entertainment distribution platforms.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Intellectual property rights face ongoing challenges as early copyrights expire, including Steamboat Willie, with revenue impacts expected from expired IP. Legal landscape uncertainty surrounds AI technologies affecting both content protection capabilities and compliance obligations, while regulatory environment remains unpredictable with evolving privacy, data protection, and content distribution requirements creating compliance cost variability.

periodic.operating_state: Total net sales increased 13.3% to $167.7 billion in Q2 2025, driven by AWS growth of 17.5% and double-digit expansion across North America and International segments with favorable foreign exchange impact. Operating income expanded 30.6% to $19.2 billion reflecting improved operational leverage across all segments. Six-month revenue reached $323.4 billion, up 11.0% year-over-year, demonstrating consistent expansion.
periodic.cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 11.4% to $148.5 billion, demonstrating improved cost leverage as expense growth lagged revenue expansion by approximately 200 basis points. Technology and infrastructure costs surged 22% primarily from AWS infrastructure investments and accelerated server depreciation, while fulfillment costs rose from increased sales volume partially offset by network efficiencies.
periodic.material_events: Amazon changed server and networking equipment useful life from six to five years effective January 2025, increasing Q2 depreciation expense by $280 million. The company invested $1.3 billion in Anthropic convertible notes with an additional $1.4 billion commitment by Q4 2025, having previously realized a $3.9 billion gain from converting notes to preferred stock in Q1 2025. Capital expenditures increased substantially to $31.4 billion from $16.4 billion primarily for technology infrastructure supporting AWS growth.
periodic.macro_exposures: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 reinstated 100% accelerated depreciation and immediate R&D expensing retroactively, expected to significantly decrease U.S. cash taxes while increasing income tax provision. Foreign exchange volatility presents ongoing risk with substantial foreign-denominated cash and securities exposed to potential declines. Increased regulatory scrutiny includes ongoing Federal Trade Commission antitrust proceedings while expanding global tax obligations include new revenue-based taxes and global minimum tax implementations.
periodic.forward_outlook: Q3 2025 guidance projects net sales between $174.0-179.5 billion representing 10-13% growth with favorable foreign exchange impact anticipated, and operating income expected between $15.5-20.5 billion. Management expects technology and infrastructure spending to increase over time supporting product and service expansion. AWS maintains substantial unrecognized performance obligations with weighted-average remaining contract life of 4.0 years.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Income tax contingencies totaled $5.8 billion with ongoing IRS examinations and multiple international tax disputes including contested Indian cloud services assessments. Substantial legal contingencies from expanding litigation portfolio include patent litigation regarding AWS Nitro System technology. Evolving artificial intelligence regulations and data protection requirements create compliance complexity, while semiconductor supply constraints for AI infrastructure components pose risks to technology development timelines.

periodic.operating_state: Total revenue declined 11% to $41.83 billion for the six months driven by automotive sales decreasing from simultaneous factory changeovers to New Model Y and lower average selling prices from sales mix and customer incentives. Energy generation and storage revenue increased from higher Megapack and Powerwall deployments despite lower pricing. Services revenue grew from increased Supercharging, maintenance, insurance, and used vehicle sales.
periodic.cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed from 17.7% to 16.8% primarily due to lower automotive margins from reduced regulatory credits revenue and pricing pressure. Automotive gross margin declined from 18.5% to 16.8% driven by lower average selling prices and decreased regulatory credits, partially offset by lower material costs. Energy storage gross margin expanded from 24.6% to 29.6% due to lower unit costs from reduced raw material expenses.
periodic.material_events: Launched Robotaxi service in Austin in June 2025 capitalizing on AI investments and mobility infrastructure. Appointed John R. Hartung to the Board of Directors effective June 1, 2025, serving on the Audit Committee. Completed restructuring actions with employee termination expenses substantially finished by Q2 2025.
periodic.macro_exposures: Trade policy uncertainty poses risks to global supply chain costs and component availability, with current tariff regime having relatively larger impact on energy business versus automotive. OBBBA provisions including removal of electric vehicle tax credits may impact consumer demand once effective. Higher import tariffs and retaliatory measures could adversely affect consumer spending and increase battery cell expenses.
periodic.forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $9.00 billion in 2025 for AI infrastructure, factory expansion, and product development, though trade policy changes may necessitate project timeline adjustments. Focus on profitable growth through leveraging existing capacity for new affordable products, advancing FSD capabilities, and expanding Robotaxi service. Next production growth phase driven by autonomy advances and next-generation platform vehicles.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Government incentive reductions or eliminations for electric vehicles and energy storage products could negatively impact demand and financial results. Net metering benefits for solar customers face potential reductions across multiple jurisdictions. Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations on significant intercompany balances create period-to-period volatility as the company typically does not hedge foreign currency risk.

periodic.operating_state: Net sales increased 4.9% to $45.3 billion in Q2 2025, driven primarily by SRS acquisition contributing incremental revenue. Comparable sales rose 1.0% reflecting 1.4% higher average ticket offset by 0.4% decline in customer transactions. Performance reflects customer engagement with smaller home improvement projects pressured by persistent high interest rates constraining large project demand.
periodic.cost_structure: Gross margin held flat at 33.4% as Primary segment benefits from shrink and supply chain efficiencies offset SRS inclusion. SG&A increased to 17.1% of sales from 16.5% due to higher payroll costs in Primary segment. Depreciation and amortization rose driven by increased intangible asset amortization from SRS acquisition.
periodic.material_events: Entered definitive agreement to acquire GMS for $110 per share representing $4.3 billion equity value, expected to close second half fiscal 2025 funded through cash and commercial paper. Completed $2.0 billion senior notes offering in September 2025 with tranches maturing 2028, 2030, and 2035. Increased quarterly dividend 2.2% to $2.30 per share and repaid $1.2 billion long-term debt during first six months.
periodic.macro_exposures: Continue monitoring tariff and trade policy developments with ongoing supply chain diversification efforts to maintain customer value proposition. Stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales by $163 million in Q2 and $437 million in first six months. High interest rate environment persists, pressuring large home improvement project demand while supporting smaller project engagement.
periodic.forward_outlook: Expect approximately $4 billion capital expenditures in fiscal 2025, representing 2.5% of net sales, focused on customer experience technology, Pro capabilities, and new stores. GMS acquisition expected to close second half fiscal 2025 subject to regulatory approvals. Share repurchases remain paused through fiscal 2025 following SRS acquisition financing.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: GMS acquisition closing contingent on regulatory approvals and majority shareholder tender. Commercial paper program expanded to $11.0 billion supported by equivalent backup credit facilities to accommodate acquisition financing. OBBBA tax law changes expected to reduce fiscal 2025 cash tax payments through enhanced expensing provisions.

periodic.operating_state: NIKE achieved record revenues of $46.7 billion in fiscal 2022, increasing 5% reported and 6% currency-neutral, driven by NIKE Direct growth of 14% to represent 42% of total revenues with digital sales reaching $10.7 billion. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in EMEA and APLA, North America growing moderately, while Greater China declined due to COVID-19 restrictions. Wholesale revenues declined as supply chain constraints most significantly affected wholesale channels, with footwear unit sales declining but average selling prices rising from higher NIKE Direct pricing and full-price mix improvements.
periodic.cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 120 basis points to 46.0% driven by NIKE Direct margin expansion, higher full-price sales mix and favorable foreign currency impacts, partially offset by elevated freight and logistics costs from supply chain disruptions and higher inventory obsolescence reserves in Greater China. Selling and administrative expenses increased due to higher strategic technology investments, wage-related expenses, NIKE Direct variable costs and normalized demand creation spending.
periodic.material_events: NIKE completed $4.0 billion in share repurchases under the four-year program and received board authorization for a new four-year $18 billion share repurchase program. The company entered definitive agreements to sell legal entities in Argentina, Uruguay and Chile to third-party distributors with closures expected by Q3 fiscal 2023. NIKE deconsolidated Russian legal entities due to economic sanctions resulting in a $96 million pre-tax charge and subsequently decided to exit the Russian marketplace entirely.
periodic.macro_exposures: COVID-19 factory shutdowns in Vietnam and Indonesia during Q1 eliminated approximately three months of production, driving inventory to $8.4 billion up 23% with extended transit times elevating in-transit inventory levels. Foreign currency translation provided favorable impact on pre-tax income through international profit translation and hedging activities, though volatility remains elevated due to geopolitical dynamics and stronger U.S. Dollar.
periodic.forward_outlook: Management expects inventory supply to significantly lag consumer demand with transportation and logistics costs remaining elevated while product costs stay high due to input cost inflation. Annual capital expenditures of approximately 3% of revenues will focus on digital capabilities, technology foundation and supply chain improvements. The company maintains multi-year investment plans for digital transformation and brand campaigns while continuing Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy execution.
periodic.uncertainty_profile: Operating environment volatility expected to continue with ongoing COVID-19 variant risks potentially disrupting operations and impacting revenue growth and profitability. Argentina subsidiary operates in hyperinflationary environment requiring U.S. Dollar functional currency with outstanding endorsement and product purchase obligations representing significant future cash commitments. Cybersecurity and climate change risks have increased requiring additional facility investments affecting raw material availability and costs.

earnings_call.summary: Nike reported Q3 FY2025 revenue down 7% currency-neutral, with NIKE Direct declining 10% and wholesale down 4%. Gross margins fell 330 basis points to 41.5% due to higher markdowns and inventory obsolescence. The company is executing five 'Win Now' priority actions focused on culture, product portfolio diversification, brand distinction, marketplace elevation, and local market presence. Performance categories like running and training showed growth, but this was offset by double-digit declines in classic footwear franchises (Air Force 1, Dunk, Air Jordan 1). Management is aggressively cleaning up marketplace inventory and repositioning NIKE Digital as a full-price business.
earnings_call.management_outlook: Management expects Q4 revenue to decline mid-teens with gross margins down 400-500 basis points, representing the largest impact from Win Now actions before headwinds begin to moderate. The company is accelerating the transition away from classic franchises, expecting them to be down 10+ percentage points as a percent of total footwear mix by Q4, with further reductions planned for FY2026. Digital traffic is expected to be down double digits in FY2026 as promotional activity is reduced. Management believes wholesale will return to growth once marketplace cleanup is complete and new product flow is established.
earnings_call.risks: The company faces significant inventory challenges across all geographies, particularly elevated Air Jordan 1 and Dunk inventory requiring continued liquidation actions through H1 FY2026. Greater China revenue declined 15% with aggressive marketplace cleanup actions negatively impacting revenue and margins. External uncertainties include geopolitical dynamics, new tariffs on imports from China and Mexico, volatile foreign exchange rates, and macro factors affecting consumer confidence. The transition away from classic franchises creates near-term revenue headwinds while new product scaling takes time.
earnings_call.key_drivers: Performance categories, particularly running (mid-single digit growth) and training, are driving momentum with successful launches of Peg Premium and Vomero 18. The company is investing heavily in brand marketing (demand creation up 8%) around major sports moments like Super Bowl and NBA All-Star weekend. Wholesale order books in North America, EMEA, and APLA show performance growth and sportswear newness nearly offsetting classic franchise declines. The integrated marketplace strategy focuses on three key countries (US, China, UK) and five cities (NYC, LA, London, Beijing, Shanghai) for concentrated investment and impact.
